content: Hailing from the picturesque state of Jharkhand, I completed my B.Tech in Electronics and Telecommunication. After graduation, I joined a service-based company, but my heart yearned for a shift towards a product-based role that would allow me to dive deeper into cutting-edge technologies and innovation. Luckily, my life turned for the better when I discovered Scaler , which played a pivotal role in my transformation. The well-structured curriculum catered to my passion for problem-solving, computer fundamentals, and software design, which are quintessential qualities for a successful software engineer. I recently joined KloudGin with Scaler Support and my hard work and dedication.

name: Suraj Patil
content: At Scaler having been taught by the best Alok S., Anshuman Singh and Prateek Narang and learning with the best peers helped me build on my DSA skills which are crucial to making it through any interview. On the other hand, it also prepared me for skills that are required to efficiently work in a job, especially system design. This is one of the major topics that I got limited exposure to before this course but I am so glad that I did. Also, with the daily assignments I got into the routine of solving problems everyday. As a result of this practice, I have come out on the other side of the tunnel much more confident

name: Megha Ganguly
content: Hi folks, want to share a personal anecdote today. Alongside my passion for coding, Ive always loved painting, which serves as a great stress reliever. Ive been painting since childhood and enjoy participating in Inktober at my workplace. Additionally, I find solace in reading Hindi novels, finding inspiration from remarkable personalities like Sunita Williams and APJ Abdul Kalam. Their stories and simplicity resonate deeply with me.

name: K Lalith Kumar
content: The secret ingredients to success are- passion and consistent effort. If youre passionate, the effort wont seem like a burden because you will do what you love. Following this ideology, I have followed my passion and never compromised on effort. Even if it was just 1% every day, I did it. As a sweet result of this, I recently joined Publicis Sapient as a senior software engineer and I would not have it any other way. Id also like to express my gratitude to Scaler for being a great supporter throughout my journey of upskilling.

name: sanju saini
content: Challenges are the stepping stones to our growth, transforming adversity into our greatest strength. And while that is true, challenges deter our progress is equally true too. For me, the dream of becoming a software engineer did not follow a smooth path. Financial constraints initially deterred me from going to an engineering college. But I was not the one to give up. I knew if I put in just enough effort, I could bridge this gap. I went in for a two-year diploma in 2019 which gave me an orientation to the field! The string of challenges, however, continued. The language of teaching was in Hindi which I could not have imagined would make such a difference. No matter what they say, communication skills play a huge role in guaranteeing success in your career. And hence began the journey of reaching my full potential.

name: Praveen
content: For seamless success I think we all need companions and peers basically a support system that can be our friend and critic as well. I was fortunate enough to find my tribe and with their support and my hard work I have landed a very good job at Ivykids (formerly Yellow Class) as SDE-I. Ivykids (formerly Yellow Class) was one of the three companies I had recently interviewed for through Scaler. Although I got through them all, Ivykids (formerly Yellow Class) seems to be the best choice in terms of many factors. This has been my first achievement since I started a journey towards improvement and progress a few months back

Yet, I never knew just how much of a difference your DSA knowledge coupled with regular practicing problems can bring about..

name: Shivam Mishra
content:

name: Shubham Bhatia
content:
My journey of transitioning into the IT field

It all started with just a spark of curiosity and transformed into a full fledged love for the field. So much so that all my efforts led me to be a professional who could crack multiple companies and offers. This is especially a rewarding feeling because working as a software engineer was all I imagined since the time I decided to transition into the IT field. 

With a background in electronics and communication, my eye was on one goal simply- upskilling in CS and giving it my all. And once the focus was set, there was no turning back. Scaler was a huge part of this decision and a huge shoutout to them for bringing it all together.

name: Amal K Ajith
content:
Hi Linkedin Fam,

This is my first post here.

I am happy to say that I have joined Impact Analytics as a SDE. I am putting this up here to show my appreciation towards Scaler and how they played a major part in my journey.

I joined Scaler back in July 2022, with the aim of upskilling and landing a good job along the way. I am glad to say, I did. The journey was tough but Scaler made it easy.

DSA was something that I was not a big fan of, but when I attended the classes, it made me think often, was DSA that easy to understand? The credit goes to Scaler and my DSA instructor Utkarsh Gupta..

name: Rohit Maurya
content:
Computer science is such a dynamic field. And with that dynamic nature comes the requirement of being consistently adaptable to new trends and changes.

To bridge the gap between what I knew and what was required in the industry as a working professional, I began my upskilling journey which recently reached its first milestone- landing at Glance a month ago. This one month has been full of learnings and adventures working in the capacity of SDE-II.

Also central to this has been the constant application of what I learned during my upskilling journey. As a result of a lot of problem-solving, the transformation of my mindset towards problems at work has completely transformed which has been visible in this one month.
